title: Content Digests, Near-Duplicate Evidence, Change Classes, and History · Runtime Contract: Exact Equivalence
summary: Canonical URLs, byte hashes, text similarity, DOM structure, and latest records are collapsed into one destructive duplicate decision. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go exact equivalence increment in multi-stage versioned content evidence store. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Distinguish URL alias, byte identity, admitted-record equality, replay, and observation duplication while retaining lineage.
Label raw, decoded, DOM, selected-content, record, and rendered digests with algorithm and transformation version.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Predict Exact Equivalence · Theory Exact Equivalence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 4408 during an owner-approved community information audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate digest stages, exact and near identity, change classes, versions, tombstones, and deletion; failed, canceled, stale, over-budget, and revoked work preserves prior admitted state; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish URL alias, byte identity, admitted-record equality, replay, and observation duplication while retaining lineage.
